edges.description: Impaired adipocyte triglyceride synthesis and storage contributes to generalized adipose tissue loss.
Failed adipocyte differentiation and lipid-droplet maintenance reduces adipose tissue mass.
intermediate_mechanisms: Seipin loss disrupts terminal adipocyte differentiation.
Lipid-droplet loss and adipose transcriptional silencing reduce white adipose tissue.

edges.description: BSCL2-associated disease has higher risk of intellectual impairment than AGPAT2-associated disease.
Defective caveola and lipid-droplet expansion impairs adipose tissue maintenance.
intermediate_mechanisms: CAV1-dependent caveolae maintain lipid-droplet integrity and expansion.
Impaired adipocyte lipid-droplet expansion reduces adipose tissue maintenance.

edges.description: Caveola biogenesis failure disrupts adipocyte lipid handling and tissue expandability.
intermediate_mechanisms: CAVIN1/PTRF is required for caveola biogenesis and stabilization.
Caveola loss impairs adipocyte differentiation and tissue expandability.

edges.description: CAVIN1/PTRF-associated type 4 disease includes congenital myopathy.
CAVIN1/PTRF-associated myopathy is accompanied by high serum creatine kinase.

Near-total adipose loss clinically manifests as generalized lipodystrophy.
Loss of adipocytes produces the HPO adipose tissue loss phenotype.
Inability to store triglyceride in adipose tissue diverts lipid to non-adipose organs.
intermediate_mechanisms: Near-total adipose loss limits efficient storage of circulating triglycerides.
Excess triglycerides spill over to liver and skeletal muscle.

edges.description: Near-total adipose tissue loss markedly reduces leptin production.
Near-total adipose tissue loss lowers circulating leptin.
Loss of subcutaneous fat and the CGL body habitus present clinically as extreme muscularity or muscle hypertrophy.
Hypertrichosis is a recurrent syndromic feature of congenital generalized lipodystrophy, but the cached evidence does not resolve a single mechanism.
Near-total subcutaneous adipose loss can make superficial veins conspicuous.
Ectopic triglyceride accumulation in hepatocytes produces hepatic steatosis.

Hepatic lipid accumulation contributes to liver enlargement.
intermediate_mechanisms: Ectopic hepatic triglyceride accumulation causes hepatic steatosis.
Hepatic steatosis can enlarge the liver.

edges.description: Impaired adipose storage and altered lipid handling contribute to circulating hypertriglyceridemia.
Broader dyslipidemia can include elevated circulating cholesterol as well as hypertriglyceridemia.
Ectopic lipid accumulation is a major driver of severe insulin resistance.
Hepatic steatosis and intramyocellular lipid accumulation contribute to the severe insulin-resistance mechanism, although the intervening molecular steps remain incompletely defined.
Severe leptin deficiency worsens metabolic insulin resistance.
intermediate_mechanisms: Hyperphagia exacerbates metabolic derangements and insulin resistance.

edges.description: Leptin deficiency worsens triglyceride dysregulation.
Worsened metabolic derangement contributes to diabetes.
The mechanism directly produces the clinical insulin-resistance phenotype.
Pancreatic compensation for insulin resistance increases circulating insulin.
Severe insulin resistance predisposes to diabetes mellitus.
Chronic insulin resistance can produce acanthosis nigricans.
Severe cardiometabolic disease can include hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, but the cached evidence does not resolve a single intermediate mechanism.
Cardiomyopathy in congenital generalized lipodystrophy can progress to clinical congestive heart failure in a subset of patients.
Elevated circulating triglycerides report the failed adipose-storage and ectopic triglyceride branch of CGL.
Low circulating leptin reports the hypoleptinemic metabolic branch caused by near-total adipose tissue loss.
Elevated circulating insulin reports compensation for severe insulin resistance.
